2603.23964.title: From Pixels to Digital Agents: An Empirical Study on the Taxonomy and Technological Trends of Reinforcement Learning Environments
2603.23964.paper_summary: The remarkable progress of reinforcement learning (RL) is intrinsically tied to the environments used to train and evaluate artificial agents. Moving beyond traditional qualitative reviews, this work presents a large-scale, data-driven empirical investigation into the evolution of RL environments. By programmatically processing a massive corpus of academic literature and rigorously distilling over 2,000 core publications, we propose a quantitative methodology to map the transition from isolated physical simulations to generalist, language-driven foundation agents. Implementing a novel, multi-dimensional taxonomy, we systematically analyze benchmarks against diverse application domains and requisite cognitive capabilities. Our automated semantic and statistical analysis reveals a profound, data-verified paradigm shift: the bifurcation of the field into a "Semantic Prior" ecosystem dominated by Large Language Models (LLMs) and a "Domain-Specific Generalization" ecosystem. Furthermore, we characterize the "cognitive fingerprints" of these distinct domains to uncover the underlying mechanisms of cross-task synergy, multi-domain interference, and zero-shot generalization. Ultimately, this study offers a rigorous, quantitative roadmap for designing the next generation of Embodied Semantic Simulators, bridging the gap between continuous physical control and high-level logical reasoning.
2603.23964.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）的顯著進展與用於訓練和評估人工代理的環境密切相關。<lb/>超越傳統的定性評估，本研究提出了一項大規模、數據驅動的實證調查，探討RL環境的演變。<lb/>通過程序化處理大量的學術文獻，並嚴格提煉出2000多篇核心出版物，我們提出了一種定量方法論，以映射從孤立的物理模擬到通用的、以語言驅動的基礎代理的過渡。<lb/>實施一種新穎的多維分類法，我們系統地分析了針對多樣應用領域和所需認知能力的基準。<lb/>我們的自動語義和統計分析揭示了一個深刻的、數據驗證的範式轉變：該領域分化為一個由大型語言模型（LLMs）主導的「語義先驗」生態系統和一個「特定領域的通用化」生態系統。<lb/>此外，我們還特徵化了這些不同領域的「認知指紋」，以揭示跨任務協同、多領域干擾和零樣本通用化的潛在機制。<lb/>最終，本研究提供了一個嚴謹的、定量的路線圖，用於設計下一代具身語義模擬器，彌合連續物理控制與高級邏輯推理之間的鴻溝。
2603.23964.author: Lijing Luo et.al.
2603.23964.authors: Lijing Luo, Yiben Luo, Alexey Gorbatovski, Sergey Kovalchuk, Xiaodan Liang
